Farn Pollrtcal PnacrICES CoruvtIssIoN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322'5660 ' Fax (916) 322-0886
January 26,2006
Robert Colven
Big Bear City
P.O. Box 2025
Big Bear City, CA 92314
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-240
Dear Mr. Colven:
This letter confirms the receipt of your November 17 , 2005, request for advice
regarding the actions of a "serving director" in connection with a mailer. We also
obtained further information from you regarding your letter during a telephone
conversation on January 10,2006, in which you clarified that you were not requesting
information on behalf of a Director of the Big Bear Community Services District, nor
with regard to a future governmental action.
Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8XB),
unless the public official has authorized you as his or her representative, the Fair Political
practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. Also, under the
provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(bX8)(A), the Commission
declines to provide advice regarding past conduct. I have enclosed a copy ofregulation
18329 for your review.
If you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you may file
the enclosed complaint form with our Enforcement Division. Also, please consult our
website (www.fopc.ca.eov) for detailed information about the Political Reform Act.


File No. 05-240
Page No. 2
Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Andreas C. Rockas
Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Under California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B), unless the public official has authorized the requestor as his or her representative, the FPPC cannot address the questions or provide analysis. Under section 18329(b)(8)(A), the Commission declines to provide advice regarding past conduct.
sections.question_synthetic: Mr. Colven requested advice regarding actions of a serving director in connection with a mailer, but upon clarification, indicated the request was neither on behalf of a Director nor regarding a future governmental action.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requestor was not authorized as a representative of the public official and the request involved past conduct rather than prospective guidance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter where FPPC refused to provide advice on jurisdictional grounds. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections were present; language was extracted from body of letter. Document clearly indicates why advice was declined.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Colven requested advice regarding actions of a serving director in connection with a mailer, but upon clarification, indicated the request was neither on behalf of a Director nor regarding a future governmental action.

CONCLUSION: Under California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B), unless the public official has authorized the requestor as his or her representative, the FPPC cannot address the questions or provide analysis. Under section 18329(b)(8)(A), the Commission declines to provide advice regarding past conduct.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for advice from Mr. Colven regarding a serving director's actions in connection with a mailer, citing lack of authorization and the request's focus on past conduct rather than future governmental action.
